design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Leave-one-cluster sign-flip phase for two-way fixed-effect event-study ATT estimates. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille negative-weight decompositions, Sun-Abraham event-study contamination, and cluster jackknife influence diagnostics; closest banked proposals are q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov and eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1. Our theorem is not that because it is not another negative-weight decomposition or standard influence-function statement: it gives an exact finite leverage table where the full TWFE/event-study contrast is positive but the leave-one-cluster jackknife contrast is negative, and derives the sharp threshold in the residualized cluster leverage h_g and signed cohort-time contrast s_g. Why non-trivial: the sign flip occurs without changing treatment effects, only by deleting one cluster and recomputing residualized weights. Why promising: the concrete object is a hand-specified 4 cluster by 4 period adoption table with residualized treatment column, cluster deletion Sherman-Morrison update, and a threshold inequality s_g h_g greater than the full contrast margin. Reject if the delta reduces to ordinary negative weights, support coverage, constrained-gradient geometry, or routine jackknife algebra without the finite witness table.

proposed_from.topic: Leave-one-cluster sign-flip phase for two-way fixed-effect event-study ATT estimates. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille negative-weight decompositions, Sun-Abraham event-study contamination, and cluster jackknife influence diagnostics; closest banked proposals are q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov and eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1. Our theorem is not that because it is not another negative-weight decomposition or standard influence-function statement: it gives an exact finite leverage table where the full TWFE/event-study contrast is positive but the leave-one-cluster jackknife contrast is negative, and derives the sharp threshold in the residualized cluster leverage h_g and signed cohort-time contrast s_g. Why non-trivial: the sign flip occurs without changing treatment effects, only by deleting one cluster and recomputing residualized weights. Why promising: the concrete object is a hand-specified 4 cluster by 4 period adoption table with residualized treatment column, cluster deletion Sherman-Morrison update, and a threshold inequality s_g h_g greater than the full contrast margin. Reject if the delta reduces to ordinary negative weights, support coverage, constrained-gradient geometry, or routine jackknife algebra without the finite witness table.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue entries are Q1 SignFlip, which proves an iid Bernoulli switch-on sign threshold, and Q1 SpectralThreshold, which refutes a Markov scalar spectral sign rule. The banked eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1 attempt is closer in spirit but failed because estimator matrices and influence contrasts were not derived. This proposal instead asks for a concrete equal-target four-cluster event-study deletion witness with computed TWFE and modern ATT contrasts.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (negative TWFE weights), Goodman-Bacon (full-sample timing decomposition), Sun-Abraham (event-study contamination), BJS and Callaway-Sant'Anna (modern ATT comparators), and MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb (cluster leverage diagnostics). The epsilon-gap is that none gives a finite equal-target refitted leave-one-cluster table where TWFE changes sign while modern ATT estimators remain positive."}
seed_list: Introduce the LOCO frontier J_g=beta s_g h_g/(1-h_g) and conjecture it is necessary and sufficient for a positive TWFE event-study contrast to turn negative after deleting cluster g.
Derive primitive loading rows for TWFE, BJS, Sun-Abraham, Callaway-Sant'Anna, and Wooldridge estimators and classify deletions that change influence projection without changing the ATT target.
Construct a four-cluster by four-period equal-target witness where TWFE flips after deleting one high-leverage cluster while BJS and interaction-weighted ATT estimators remain positive.
Develop a selective-inference bound for the event that at least one leave-one-cluster TWFE event-study contrast crosses zero after diagnostic inspection.
Decompose lead-coefficient deletion sign changes into residualized leverage, Sun-Abraham contamination, and pretest-selection components.
Characterize the smallest adoption-support loss under which deleting a cluster changes the event-study estimand rather than only the projection weights.
Define a cluster deletion stability index comparing TWFE and modern ATT estimators and conjecture an estimator-ordering phase in the index.
Give an open-neighborhood version of the four-cluster witness so the deletion sign flip is not a single hand-tuned table.
Construct a corrected TWFE diagnostic that subtracts the LOCO leverage term and conjecture it preserves the full-sample sign under positive ATT tables.
Derive a finite algorithm that enumerates all one-cluster deletion sign phases from a cohort-time residualized design matrix.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce the LOCO frontier J_g=beta s_g h_g/(1-h_g) and conjecture it is necessary and sufficient for a positive TWFE event-study contrast to turn negative after deleting cluster g.

anchor_paper.author: q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov reviewer/banked proposal plus Goodman-Bacon/de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille/MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb
anchor_paper.claim: Existing full-sample TWFE and cluster-leverage results do not give a recomputed deletion sign frontier.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: withdraws any claim that the sign of FWL weights is determined by (rho_T)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-FWL risk; stronger because the focal object is the deletion-recomputed LOCO frontier with a finite witness table.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from negative TWFE weights plus ordinary jackknife leverage because deletion influence is a standard linear-model calculation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The natural attempt fails because the fixed effects and residualized event column are recomputed after deleting a whole cluster, changing the design rather than evaluating a fixed full-sample influence function.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Goodman-Bacon or de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille because they decompose the full-sample TWFE estimand, while this characterizes a refit deletion frontier in h_g and s_g.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine block-inversion algebra creates a new focal object, J_g, and an exhibited finite phase table rather than reusing negative weights.

adversarial_referee.instance: Panel flagship might lack estimator/inference duality beyond the diagnostic functional.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The regression estimator is the named TWFE event-study contrast and the result gives a computable diagnostic frontier; inference is scoped out.

adversarial_referee.instance: Cluster leverage diagnostics may already imply the frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb diagnose leverage for clustered regressions but do not refit event-study fixed effects after cluster deletion.

adversarial_referee.instance: The four-cluster table could be a hand-tuned corner.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal includes nonzero denominators, positive leverage, negative deleted sign, and an open-neighborhood conjecture.

seed_details.one_line: Derive primitive loading rows for TWFE, BJS, Sun-Abraham, Callaway-Sant'Anna, and Wooldridge estimators and classify deletions that change influence projection without changing the ATT target.

anchor_paper.author: eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior estimator-frontier matrices were rejected because they were not derived entrywise from estimator equations.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Define E_m and Z_m entrywise for BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends estimator comparisons; stronger only if entrywise loading derivation yields a target-vs-projection classification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the loading-matrix repair requested by the previous reviewer.
seed_details.rebuttal: It becomes new only when paired with the deletion classification; otherwise it is a support lemma, not the main angle.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier because it derives estimator loadings from equations and asks which deletion changes projection versus target.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the deletion classification is sharpened into a necessary-and-sufficient theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a four-cluster by four-period equal-target witness where TWFE flips after deleting one high-leverage cluster while BJS and interaction-weighted ATT estimators remain positive.

anchor_paper.author: eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier reviewer plus Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe
anchor_paper.claim: The missing object is equal-target unequal-influence separation, not another TWFE negative-weight example.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: TWFE decomposition/negative weights, not equal-target unequal-IF projections

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample risk; stronger if promoted to an open neighborhood and tied to modern ATT estimators.
seed_details.referee_objection: A single finite witness is not publishable after Goodman-Bacon and Sun-Abraham.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone it is field-tier; as the SC10 exhibit for the LOCO frontier it supports the flagship seed.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a selective-inference bound for the event that at least one leave-one-cluster TWFE event-study contrast crosses zero after diagnostic inspection.

anchor_paper.author: Conley-Taber, MacKinnon-Webb, Pustejovsky-Tipton, Abadie-Athey-Imbens-Wooldridge
anchor_paper.claim: Few-cluster and design-based inference is delicate, but the selected sign-flip event is not a standard cluster-robust statistic.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: DiD inference is nonstandard when the number of policy-changing groups is small.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity/inference extension; weak without a concrete selective statistic and finite-sample law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is selective inference applied to a max-over-deletions statistic.
seed_details.rebuttal: A publishable version would need a new polyhedral or finite-cluster bound; absent that object it is below flagship.

seed_details.one_line: Decompose lead-coefficient deletion sign changes into residualized leverage, Sun-Abraham contamination, and pretest-selection components.

anchor_paper.author: Sun-Abraham, Roth, Freyaldenhoven-Hansen-Shapiro, Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: Lead coefficients confound treatment-effect contamination, pretrend interpretation, and diagnostic deletion leverage.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Apparent pretrends in event-study regressions can arise solely from treatment-effect heterogeneity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; stronger only if the three components are separately identified in one finite formula.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a survey-style decomposition of known pretrend issues.
seed_details.rebuttal: The deletion leverage term is new, but the selection component makes the kernel too broad for v1.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the smallest adoption-support loss under which deleting a cluster changes the event-study estimand rather than only the projection weights.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway-Sant'Anna and Sun-Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Modern event-study estimators depend on group-time support, so deletion may alter target support rather than only influence.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: identify and aggregate group-time ATT parameters in multiple-period DiD

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; weaker because support-loss conditions may be routine support coverage.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just support overlap after deleting a cluster.
seed_details.rebuttal: The exact target-vs-projection distinction is useful but likely field-tier unless coupled to the LOCO frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Define a cluster deletion stability index comparing TWFE and modern ATT estimators and conjecture an estimator-ordering phase in the index.

anchor_paper.author: Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe
anchor_paper.claim: The modern DiD synthesis distinguishes problematic TWFE practice from heterogeneity-robust estimators.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: distinguishes problematic TWFE practice from heterogeneity-robust staggered-adoption estimators

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object index; weak if it is a relabelled influence norm.
seed_details.referee_objection: Estimator stability indices are generic diagnostics, not theorem-level econometrics.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is a possible corollary of the LOCO frontier, not a flagship seed by itself.

seed_details.one_line: Give an open-neighborhood version of the four-cluster witness so the deletion sign flip is not a single hand-tuned table.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille and prior eid_bjs reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Negative TWFE examples and prior witnesses need promotion beyond single-instance tables.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: explicit 3-cohort x 4-period witness where the four estimators identify the same ATT

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness-trivial risk; stronger because open-neighborhood promotion directly answers the reviewer floor.
seed_details.referee_objection: Open-neighborhood promotion follows by continuity once the witness is nondegenerate.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is a necessary support result, not the main theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a corrected TWFE diagnostic that subtracts the LOCO leverage term and conjecture it preserves the full-sample sign under positive ATT tables.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE decompositions motivate corrected estimators or diagnostics when signed weights contaminate interpretation.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: weights may be negative, so signs can reverse even when effects are positive

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct; stronger if E* is explicit, weak if only subtracting an assumed frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: The correction is algebraic once J_g is defined.
seed_details.rebuttal: It should be deferred until the frontier is proved; otherwise it is promissory.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a finite algorithm that enumerates all one-cluster deletion sign phases from a cohort-time residualized design matrix.

anchor_paper.author: MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb and Kline-Saggio-Solvsten
anchor_paper.claim: Leave-out and leverage machinery exists for linear models, but not as an event-study sign-phase enumerator.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: cluster leverage, partial leverage, influence, and jackknife diagnostics

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability; weaker if it is just looping over clusters and running OLS.
seed_details.referee_objection: Enumeration over G deletions is computational bookkeeping.
seed_details.rebuttal: Needs the closed-form LOCO frontier to be nonroutine; otherwise field-tier.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE) decomposes staggered TWFE DiD into timing comparisons, anchoring the full-sample weight comparator.
2. de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) show negative TWFE weights can reverse signs, but not after refitting on a deleted-cluster design.
3. Sun and Abraham (2021, JoE) show dynamic TWFE contamination across relative periods.
4. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, ReStud), Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, JoE), and Wooldridge (2023, EctJ) provide heterogeneity-robust event-study comparators.
5. MacKinnon, Nielsen, and Webb (2023, Stata Journal) define leverage, influence, and jackknife diagnostics for clustered regressions.
6. Kline, Saggio, and Solvsten (2020, Econometrica) provide leave-out linear-model machinery for variance components, not event-study sign phases.
7. Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe (2023, JoE) frame modern DiD practice and motivate diagnostics separating TWFE fragility from target movement.
8. The mined q1_spectral_phase_transition proposal withdrew scalar spectral sign claims, showing that sign rules need additional balance/leverage coordinates.
9. The eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier reviewer flagged that estimator loading matrices must be derived entrywise, not stipulated.
10. The active gap is a finite leave-one-cluster sign-flip phase law for residualized TWFE/event-study contrasts, not another negative-weight decomposition or jackknife variance formula.
